back_translation: And it happened afterward, that David struck the Philistines and subdued them, and David took Metheg-ammah from the hand of the Philistines.

And he struck Moab, and measured them with a line, making them lie down to the ground; and he measured off two lines to put to death, and the full length of the line to keep alive. And Moab became servants to David, bringing tribute.
And David struck Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah, when he went to restore his hand at the river of the Euphrates.
And David captured from him a thousand and seven hundred horsemen, and twenty thousand footmen; and David hamstrung all the chariot horses and kept of them a hundred chariots.
And Aram of Damascus came to help Hadadezer king of Zobah, and David struck of Aram twenty-two thousand men.
And David put garrisons in Aram of Damascus, and Aram became servants to David, bringing tribute. And the LORD saved David in everywhere he went.
And David took the shields of gold that were upon the servants of Hadadezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.
And from Betah and from Berothai, cities of Hadadezer, King David took bronze in great abundance.
And Toi king of Hamath heard that David had struck all the army of Hadadezer.

And Toi sent Joram his son to King David, to ask him for peace and to bless him because he had fought against Hadadezer and struck him — for Hadadezer was a man of wars of Toi. And in his hand were articles of silver and articles of gold and articles of bronze.
These also King David dedicated to the LORD, with the silver and the gold which he dedicated from all the nations he subdued —
from Aram, and from Moab, and from the sons of Ammon, and from the Philistines, and from Amalek, and from the spoil of Hadadezer son of Rehob king of Zobah.
And David made a name when he returned from his striking Aram in the Valley of Salt, eighteen thousand.
And he placed garrisons in Edom; in all Edom he placed garrisons. And all Edom became servants to David. And the LORD saved David in everywhere he went.
And David reigned over all Israel; and David was administering judgment and righteousness for all his people.
And Joab son of Zeruiah was over the army, and Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder.
And Zadok son of Ahitub and Ahimelech son of Abiathar were priests, and Seraiah was scribe.
And Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and the sons of David were priests.
